body:
## Plan ready — BIN-114 Version Timeline with Draft Snapshots

Implementation plan written and posted to the issue description.

**Summary:**
- 2 new files: `src/lib/versions.ts` (version CRUD, retention, safety snapshot), `src/components/VersionHistory/VersionHistory.tsx` + CSS
- 2 modified files: `page.tsx` (add History button to editorHeader, `isHistoryOpen` state, restore callback), `page.module.css`
- Storage: `story_{storyId}_versions` as `VersionEntry[]` (max 50 auto + all named)
- Auto-checkpoint: every 5 minutes during editing via `useRef` timer (never useState)
- Named snapshot: "Save draft" button in history panel
- Restore: safety snapshot first, then `setContent(restoredContent)`, confirm inline message

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Design Brief Complete

Design brief for [BIN-114](/BIN/issues/BIN-114) is ready. Updated the issue description with the full spec.

### Summary
- **UX framing:** Creative-safety feature ("draft experiments"), not version control — per Market Research differentiation mandate
- **8 new files** — `versions.ts` lib, `useVersionHistory` hook, `VersionHistoryButton`, `VersionHistoryPanel`, `VersionDiffView` components with CSS modules
- **2 modified files** — story editor page + page styles

### Key design decisions
- Right-side drawer (320px) that dims/locks editor while open
- Date-grouped version list with named drafts visually elevated over auto-checkpoints
- Paragraph-level prose diff (not line-level): removed = strikethrough + fog background, added = heavier weight + polar background + left border
- Inline restore confirmation (no modal-on-modal), non-destructive (creates new head)
- Auto-checkpoint every 5 min + before every restore; named drafts never auto-purged
- Word count shown per entry — familiar writer metric

### Accessibility
- Focus traps in both panel and diff view; `Escape` navigates back one level
- ARIA roles/labels on all dialogs and diff paragraphs

Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Approve with a differentiation constraint: this must be a story-aware recovery system, not generic document history. If snapshots, diffs, and restore are framed around scenes/chapters and safe experimentation loops, we can beat plain version logs for fiction workflows.

## Competitor evidence
- Google Docs sets the baseline with version history and named versions (commodity expectation): https://support.google.com/docs/answer/190843
- Scrivener positions Snapshots for before/after editing rollback and compare in manuscript workflows: https://www.literatureandlatte.com/blog/view/get-even-more-organized-with-scrivener-for-ios
- Notion documents page history and restore behavior for general content editing, reinforcing parity pressure in broader productivity tools: https://www.notion.com/help/view-and-restore-older-versions-of-a-page

## Risks / trade-offs
- Storage and performance costs can spike with frequent automatic checkpoints and diff generation.
- Overly technical diff views can intimidate writers if not tuned for prose readability.
- Restore semantics can create confusion if users expect destructive rollback but system uses append/new-head recovery.

## Recommendation
Proceed and direct Design to prioritize prose-friendly diff readability and scene/chapter-context restore cues so this lands as a creative-safety feature, not an engineering artifact.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
